title: Scroll, Masonry, Holo, Ripple & Spring
description: Five live UI/UX demos: scroll-driven stagger, CSS native masonry, holographic type tilt, water ripple canvas, spring island nav.

drops.title: Scroll-Driven Stagger
drops.description: CSS animation-timeline: view() staggered cards — each enters from a unique angle as it scrolls into view.
drops.concept: Uses the native CSS Scroll-Driven Animations API (Baseline 2024) to give each card its own view() timeline. Cards fly in from alternating directions with blur and scale, driven purely by scroll position — zero JS for the animation itself. An IntersectionObserver fallback handles older browsers.

interaction.mouse: Scroll the page to trigger card entrance animations

drops.title: CSS Native Masonry Grid
drops.description: grid-template-rows: masonry in a single CSS rule — the Pinterest layout shipping natively in Chrome 131 and Firefox.
drops.concept: CSS Grid Masonry (originally proposed by Rachel Andrew) is now shipping without flags in Chrome 131+ and Firefox 128+. Cards of varying heights pack into columns automatically. The demo shows 12 cards with procedurally drawn gradient art, and displays a browser-support banner when the spec isn't available, falling back to CSS column-count.
drops.source: MDN Web Docs — CSS Grid Masonry Layout

drops.title: Holographic Letter Tilt
drops.description: Letters respond to cursor proximity with 3D perspective tilt and rainbow holographic shimmer using CSS custom properties.
drops.concept: Each letter independently tracks the cursor's distance and direction using getBoundingClientRect(). Close letters tilt toward the cursor using CSS perspective transforms (rotateX/rotateY) while a rainbow gradient angle rotates to match. A shine overlay adds the holographic foil effect. Lerped animation prevents jitter. On touch, the last touch position drives the effect.
drops.source: Basement Studio / Emil Kowalski — holographic card effects

interaction.mouse: Move cursor near letters to tilt and shimmer them

drops.title: Water Ripple Canvas
drops.description: Click or drag to create 2D wave-equation ripples that interfere constructively and destructively in real time.
drops.concept: A finite-difference 2D wave equation simulation (the same math as Evan Wallace's WebGL water) runs on a low-resolution Uint8Array grid scaled up to fill the viewport. Each simulation step applies: h[i] = (neighbors sum * 0.5) - h_prev[i] * damping. Multiple ripple sources interfere visually, creating moiré patterns and constructive amplitude peaks. Runs at native resolution × 0.5 for 60fps on mobile.

interaction.mouse: Click or drag to create ripples

drops.title: Spring Island Navigation
drops.description: Floating bottom nav with a spring-physics indicator that overshoots and settles — no CSS transitions, pure JS spring.
drops.concept: The active-tab indicator is positioned using a real spring differential equation (a = (target-pos)*k/m - v*d/m) ticked each rAF frame. This gives it a natural overshoot and settle that CSS cubic-bezier can't replicate for arbitrary target changes. Tab icons scale up with a CSS spring easing; labels fade in beneath. The whole bar is glassmorphism with backdrop-blur.

interaction.mouse: Click tabs to move the spring indicator
